description: Stamina is the measure of a character's physical endurance. Every physical action consumes stamina. When depleted, the character cannot perform stamina-costing actions until it regenerates.
rule: If a skill does not define a stamina cost, it does not affect stamina. Skills that drain continuously (like walk) consume stamina per second. Skills with instant effects (like jump) consume a flat amount on activation.

max_stamina.note: Max stamina can be increased through vitality allocations.

regeneration.condition: Active when the character is NOT performing a stamina-consuming action.
regeneration.note: Stamina regenerates passively. Walking or jumping pauses regeneration during the action.

depletion.effect: Character cannot activate any skill with stamina cost > 0.
depletion.walk_override: Walking stops when stamina hits 0 (character cannot move).
depletion.note: There is no 'exhaustion' penalty — stamina simply stops regenerating until the action stops.

per_second.description: Continuous drain while the action is held/active.

engine_mapping.godot: PlayerController tracks current_stamina. process_movement() deducts walk cost. Jump deducts on activation. Regen applied each frame when no cost is active.
engine_mapping.agnostic: Any engine tracks stamina as a float [0, max_stamina]. Skills declare costs in their atoms. The interpreter enforces the check.
